results.label: Fever, infection, or sepsis
results.context: General medicine Adult Fever, infection, or sepsis

safety.label: Measure blood pressure
safety.reason: Fever, infection, or sepsis: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.
safety.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
safety.management_change: Document current BP and compare with baseline; hypotension or MAP concern changes sepsis escalation and fluid or vasopressor urgency.
safety.options: ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available
safety.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safety.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.

safety.label: Measure heart rate
safety.reason: Fever, infection, or sepsis: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.
safety.diagnostic_target: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, or acute physiologic stress
safety.management_change: Document tachycardia, bradycardia, or rhythm concern; abnormal rate changes acuity, volume assessment, medication review, and sepsis risk framing.
safety.options: ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available
safety.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safety.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.

safety.label: Measure respiratory rate
safety.reason: Fever, infection, or sepsis: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.
safety.diagnostic_target: tachypnea, hypoventilation, respiratory compensation, or acute illness severity
safety.management_change: Document RR directly; tachypnea can signal pneumonia, hypoxemic respiratory failure, metabolic stress, or sepsis physiology.
safety.options: ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available
safety.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safety.limitations: Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.

safety.label: Measure oxygen saturation
safety.reason: Fever, infection, or sepsis: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.
safety.diagnostic_target: hypoxemia, oxygen requirement, respiratory support need, or cardiopulmonary acuity
safety.management_change: Document room-air or oxygen-supported SpO2; hypoxemia changes respiratory support, chest imaging urgency, and ED or inpatient escalation.
safety.options: ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available
safety.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safety.limitations: Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.

safety.label: Measure temperature
safety.reason: Fever, infection, or sepsis: Temperature confirms fever or hypothermia and helps frame infection, inflammatory illness, endocrine crisis, or treatment urgency.
safety.diagnostic_target: fever, hypothermia, inflammatory illness, infection, or endocrine-crisis physiology
safety.management_change: Confirm fever or hypothermia and document method; hypothermia or very high fever changes acuity and diagnostic framing.
safety.options: ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available
safety.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safety.limitations: Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.

safety.label: Mental status
safety.reason: Sepsis severity or CNS/metabolic involvement: new altered mentation, unsafe cooperation, or deviation from baseline. This basic acuity check helps identify severity, airway/safety risk, escalation needs, and safe interpretation of the selected validated workup.
safety.diagnostic_target: Sepsis severity or CNS/metabolic involvement: new altered mentation, unsafe cooperation, or deviation from baseline.
safety.management_change: New altered mental status changes sepsis escalation, airway/safety evaluation, delirium/metabolic workup, monitoring level, and disposition.
safety.options: Baseline / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess
safety.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
safety.limitations: Mental status is affected by baseline cognition, sedatives, intoxication, pain, language barriers, hypoxia, metabolic disease, and neurologic disease; interpret with glucose, oxygenation, vitals, labs, and collateral history.

history.reason: Establishes fever reliability, tempo, pretreatment, medication masking, and host-risk context.
history.management_change: Changes urgency, culture interpretation, antimicrobial timing, medication safety review, and threshold for escalation.

Ask what antipyretics, antibiotics, steroids, or immunosuppressants were already taken.

history.label: What symptoms localize the fever source: cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, sore throat, ear or sinus pain, dental pain, dysuria, flank pain, abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, wound, line concern, headache, neck stiffness, hot joint, back pain, confusion, low urine output, fainting, or rapid worsening?
history.reason: Localizes the most likely infectious source and identifies sepsis severity clues.

history.management_change: Positive answers change urgency, vital sign reassessment, sepsis labs, cultures, fluids, antimicrobial timing, ED or inpatient escalation, and safety-netting.
history.lr_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.

core.label: Observe work of breathing
core.reason: Document tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, speech limitation, or apparent respiratory distress.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory severity: tachypnea, increased work of breathing, speech limitation, or worsening oxygen need.
core.management_change: Increased work of breathing changes oxygen strategy, chest imaging urgency, respiratory support, ED or ICU escalation, and reassessment frequency.
core.options: Normal / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.limitations: Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.

core.label: Auscultate posterior lung fields
core.reason: Listen for focal crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, or asymmetry suggesting pneumonia or another pulmonary source.
core.diagnostic_target: Respiratory fever source: pneumonia, aspiration, bronchospasm, effusion, edema, or asymmetric ventilation.
core.management_change: Abnormal lung sounds change chest imaging review, respiratory testing, oxygen/support, antimicrobial framing, bronchodilator treatment, isolation, and escalation.
core.options: Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished / Asymmetric / Unable
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.limitations: Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.

core.label: Inspect skin for infection source
core.reason: Look for rash, cellulitis, abscess, wound, drainage, ulcer, petechiae, purpura, line-site inflammation, bite, or rapidly spreading soft-tissue findings.
core.diagnostic_target: Skin, line, wound, soft-tissue, or rash source of fever, including high-risk purpura or necrotizing infection clues.
core.management_change: A skin, wound, or line source changes culture target, source control, antimicrobial coverage, wound care, isolation, and escalation.
core.options: No focal skin source / Rash / Cellulitis / Abscess / Wound or drainage / Line-site inflammation / Petechiae or purpura / Unable
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.limitations: A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.

core.label: Inspect oropharynx
core.reason: Look for pharyngeal erythema, exudate, tonsillar asymmetry, oral ulcers, dental source, thrush, mucosal lesions, or dehydration clues.
core.diagnostic_target: HEENT or oral source: pharyngitis, tonsillitis, dental infection, mucosal infection, thrush, dehydration, or airway-adjacent clue.
core.management_change: Abnormal oropharyngeal findings change testing, isolation, antimicrobial framing, hydration strategy, airway concern, dental or ENT referral, and escalation.
core.options: Normal / Erythema / Exudate / Dental concern / Oral lesion / Dry mucosa / Unable
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.limitations: Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.

core.label: Palpate radial pulses
core.reason: Assess strength, regularity, equality, and whether pulses feel thready or bounding in the setting of fever.
core.diagnostic_target: Perfusion or circulatory stress clue: weak, thready, bounding, irregular, or asymmetric pulses in fever.
core.management_change: Abnormal pulses change urgency of perfusion reassessment, fluids, lactate or shock evaluation, ECG review when irregular, and escalation.
core.options: Strong and equal / Weak / Bounding / Irregular / Asymmetric / Unable
core.lr_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
core.limitations: Pulse quality is subjective and can be affected by vascular disease, arrhythmia, temperature, anxiety, examiner skill, and cuff timing; use with BP, mental status, capillary refill or perfusion exam, and labs.

tests.label: Sepsis severity labs and reference thresholds
tests.reason: Objective severity data is needed when fever may represent sepsis; lactate >2 mmol/L, lactate >=4 mmol/L, hypotension, or new organ dysfunction changes urgency and monitoring.
tests.diagnostic_target: Sepsis severity labs and reference thresholds: objective test, threshold, imaging, or baseline data that confirms diagnosis, identifies complications, or routes care for Fever, infection, or sepsis.
tests.management_change: Consider CBC with differential, CMP or creatinine, glucose when indicated, lactate when sepsis or hypoperfusion is possible, and review prior cultures or recent antimicrobials.

tests.label: Source-directed infection studies

red_flags.label: Sepsis or shock physiology
red_flags.reason: Sepsis and septic shock are medical emergencies; bedside severity findings should accelerate cultures, lactate and organ dysfunction assessment, antimicrobials, fluids or vasopressors as indicated, and reassessment.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: Sepsis or shock physiology: danger pattern, severity marker, or complication that changes urgency for Fever, infection, or sepsis.
red_flags.management_change: Escalate urgently for ED, sepsis pathway, monitored setting, or local emergency workflow when hypotension, altered mental status, oliguria, mottling, severe weakness, hypoxemia, or rapid worsening is present.
red_flags.lr_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
red_flags.limitations: Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Fever, infection, or sepsis.

red_flags.label: CNS, airway, purpura, or necrotizing soft-tissue danger pattern
